text: Deep technical session to validate Quasar Systems' on-prem inference strategy. Focus: interaction between KV cache prefetch strategies (wavefront/pipeline), kernel selection heuristics, and CUDA Graph replay behavior across TRT-LLM and vLLM runtimes. Primary goals: identify stability knobs for CUDA Graph replay, quantify latency variability with prefetch wavefront, and outline pilot instrumentation for on-prem NVLink cluster.

Ran through a simulated degraded scenario and validated handoffs between SEs, release, and ops.

Topics covered:
- Communication tree and customer bulleting cadence
- Live-run simulation results for canary 2
- Risk #1: kernel fallback causing 2x tail latency at 95th pct
- Risk #2: cold-start KV cache warming causing token-cost spike
- Risk #3: SSO + SAML flakiness on private deployments during region failover
- Rollback vs degrade decisions and automated fallback triggers

text: Deep technical troubleshooting session focused on nondeterministic kernel switches under high-concurrency workloads. We replicated a 95th-percentile latency regression tied to CUDA-graph replay + persistent KV cache interactions on a multi-gpu on-prem cluster. Discussed root-cause hypotheses (warp scheduling, kernel fallback, quantization-induced math paths), proposed microbench experiments, and agreed on an instrumented bisect plan using NVTX, smallstep batch sweeps, and a TRT-LLM vs vLLM comparison under VPC-like network isolation.

text: Redwood and Nimbus reviewed pilot reliability telemetry from the last 72 hours: spikes of 502/504s correlated with token-burst traffic from a scheduled batch job. Team agreed next steps: tune client-side retry budget, add a short token-window rate limiter on the Redwood side, run a controlled load test against the canary model variant, and share runbook + cost impact. Security/procurement will get the pricing snapshot for scaling to Dedicated if needed.

Summary:
Quick demo focused on hosted conversational API — streaming behavior, tuning parameters for latency vs cost, and recommended retry/backoff patterns. Customer is evaluating for a pilot (chat triage and summarization workflows) and is concerned about latency tail, cost controls, and compliance (SOC2, VPC options).

Topics covered:
- Streaming vs synchronous generation
- Tokenization and batching tradeoffs
- KV cache/prefix caching and how it affects cost
- Retry strategies, idempotency, and client-side backoff
- Console + Optimize recommendations for cost and quantization suggestions
- Security/enterprise questions (SSO, KMS, data retention)

Team reviewed last week's experiments (three variants), observed improvements in effective hit rate and cost but noticed increased tail latency for long-context sessions. Decided on follow-ups: run 48h synthetic traffic with session-affinity enabled, ...
text: Weekly POC check-in focused on KV/prefix cache warmup heuristics. Team reviewed last week's experiments (three variants), observed improvements in effective hit rate and cost but noticed increased tail latency for long-context sessions. Decided on follow-ups: run 48h synthetic traffic with session-affinity enabled, prune low-value prefixes, and finalize TTL/eviction knobs for production pilot. SE to share bench spreadsheet and config diff; AE to update pilot timeline and pricing assumptions.

title: Sprint 3 preflight & access walkthrough
preview: Weekly POC sync focused on access handoff and preflight checklist for Sprint 3. Confirmed staging credentials, discussed VPC peering options and telemetry instrumentation. SE explained KV cache warmup and batching tweaks to address tail latency noted in last run. Security asked for KMS keyflow diagram and SOC2/ISO r...
text: Weekly POC sync focused on access handoff and preflight checklist for Sprint 3. Confirmed staging credentials, discussed VPC peering options and telemetry instrumentation. SE explained KV cache warmup and batching tweaks to address tail latency noted in last run. Security asked for KMS keyflow diagram and SOC2/ISO references. Agreed next steps: 6-hour stress run with increased sampling, enable tracing on 2 endpoints, and handoff of jumpbox credentials by 7/13.
